meta.title: The Agentic Design System — Process | Workhorse × GoDoGood
meta.description: A repeatable, productized process for building websites with AI agents — from audience research to autonomous content systems.
meta.ogDescription: A 10-step productized process for building websites with AI agents.

header.title: The Agentic Website Design System
header.subtitle: A repeatable, productized process for building websites with AI agents. Not designing pages — building a 3-layer system that evolves, publishes, and performs autonomously.

philosophy.label: Core Philosophy
philosophy.heading: You're not designing a website. You're building a 3-layer system.

layers.title: Human Layer
layers.description: Visual design, UX, emotion. The taste, restraint, and brand narrative that separates craft from AI slop. This is your moat.

layers.title: AI Agent Layer
layers.description: Dynamic content, automation, personalization. AI agents handle the execution — building, iterating, and scaling what the human layer designs.

layers.title: LLM Discovery Layer
layers.description: SEO, structured content, AI visibility. Websites built to be found — not just by Google, but by the LLMs that are increasingly how people discover information.

cards.name: Brand Intelligence Engine
cards.description: Audience + search intent research. Outputs structured JSON — personas, queries, pain points, messaging angles.

cards.name: Brand Copy Coach
cards.description: Structured, SEO-ready page copy. Feeds from research JSON to generate full site content with minimal manual refinement.

cards.name: Variant
cards.description: AI-generated UI systems — layouts, animations, components, visual language. The creative director you iterate with.

cards.name: Claude Code
cards.description: Front-end generation + system assembly. Turns design systems into production code, section by section.

cards.name: Custom AI Agents
cards.description: Blog generation, SEO velocity, auto-publishing. The autonomous content engine that keeps the site growing.

steps.label: Full Process
steps.heading: 10 steps from zero to autonomous website
steps.intro: Each step feeds into the next. The output of research becomes the input for copy, which feeds design, which feeds the build.

items.title: Audience Intelligence Layer
items.description: Run the full research flow. Extract top search intents, emotional triggers, and category language. This becomes your source of truth for everything downstream.

items.title: Copy System Generation
items.description: Feed in the JSON from research. Generate full site copy — homepage, about, services, contact, blog direction, SEO structure. Manually refine ~10–20%.

callout.text: This step replaces traditional UX writing, SEO planning, and content strategy decks.

items.title: Design System Creation
items.description: Start with the hero — paste homepage hero copy and prompt for futuristic, modern, visually striking designs. Iterate on layout variations, animation styles, and typography. Then build section by section, then build the component library.

callout.text: Variant = Creative Director. You = Taste + Editor. This is where most people fail by accepting AI slop.

items.title: System Transfer to Build
items.description: Initialize the project, set scope, and feed the first section. Paste Variant code — hero first, then sections, then full page. Focus on implementation, not redesign.
items.promptBlock:
This is the design system.
Build homepage section by section.
Focus on implementation, not redesign.

items.title: Full Page Assembly
items.description: Transfer all sections first. Don't refine early. The goal is a complete ugly version first — get everything on the page before polishing anything.

callout.text: Goal: complete ugly version first. Resist the urge to polish section-by-section.

items.title: Copy Injection Pass
items.description: Feed the final copy files (Markdown or text) and let Claude match all copy exactly to provided files, adapting layouts intelligently using the existing system.
items.promptBlock:
Match all copy exactly to provided files.
Adapt layouts intelligently using existing system.

items.title: Humanization Layer
items.description: Add photography and video, brand colors, typography system, spacing and hierarchy fixes. Use your own assets, AI image tools (optional), and reference boards.

callout.text: This is where it stops feeling like AI. This is the critical step most people skip.

items.title: Multi-Page Expansion
items.description: Feed remaining page copy and let Claude generate About, Services, Blog, and Contact pages. Apply the homepage design system consistently across all pages.
items.promptBlock: Apply homepage design system consistently across all pages.

items.title: Agentic Content Layer
items.description: Build the autonomous growth engine: AI blog writer agents, SEO content engine, and auto-publishing system. This is the differentiator — the site doesn't just launch, it grows.

items.title: Editing + Live Iteration
items.description: Click-to-edit UI inside Claude. Update copy, swap images, adjust layout — all through conversation. This replaces the traditional CMS editing experience entirely.

callout.text: This replaces the CMS UX layer (Webflow, WordPress admin). The AI becomes the editing interface.

pitfalls.label: Watch Out
pitfalls.heading: Where this process breaks — and how to fix it

cards.label: Problem 1
cards.title: AI Slop Design
cards.description: The default AI output is generic, overdesigned, and instantly recognizable as machine-generated.
cards.fix: Aggressive iteration in Variant. Reduce typography chaos. Enforce grid + system. Never accept the first output.

cards.label: Problem 2
cards.title: Over-automation
cards.description: Skipping the human review step and shipping raw AI output leads to sites that feel hollow and generic.
cards.fix: Insert a human pass BEFORE launch. Every time. The Humanization Layer (Step 7) is non-negotiable.

cards.label: Problem 3
cards.title: Weak Brand POV
cards.description: AI can generate layouts and copy, but it can't generate a point of view. Without strong brand narrative, the site is wallpaper.
cards.fix: Stronger narrative layer. This is your specialty — editorial taste, brand narrative, restraint, hierarchy.

cards.title: The WRKHRS "Brand Engine" Product
cards.rightText: "We build living brand systems that evolve, publish, and perform autonomously"

cards.title: Your Moat
cards.intro: This system is missing one thing that can't be automated:
